text.title: QA Risk Matrix Examples
text.summary: Worked examples — one realistic refresh and one antipattern caught by the validator.

example.title: SaaS billing-search product quarterly refresh
example.note: Q2 refresh: 12 areas enumerated. billing-subscription (impact 5, likelihood 4; HH; rationale: revenue loss within hours), auth (impact 5, likelihood 3; HH), tenant-isolation (impact 5, likelihood 1; HL; rationale: regulatory fine), search-and-filtering (impact 3, likelihood 4; LH), admin-audit-log (impact 1, likelihood 1; LL). Likelihood scores computed by scripts/likelihood-from-incidents.py against the past 6 months of incidents and commits. Investment plan: HH gets full pyramid + monthly exploratory + alerting (owner Ruslan); HL gets unit + integration + targeted exploratory; LH automation only; LL monitor-only. capacity_check shows required 32h vs team 40h: passes. Matrix published; index in QA wiki.

example.title: Flat-impact matrix rejected
example.note: First-draft matrix from a new lead: 8 areas all scored impact 5 (no customer-effect rationale), likelihood scores all in 3-4 range without likelihood_data. Investment_plan.HH says "everything important" with no owner. Validator: f1 (flat-impact), f2 (likelihood-without-data), f4 (HH without investment entry shape). Lead reworks against the customer-effect rubric: scores spread out to a 5/4/3/3/3/2/2/1 distribution; likelihood_data populated from incident log; investment plan rewritten with owners and capacity_check.
